name: PSLV-XL
first: Most reliable medium launcher in service — flew Chandrayaan-1 (2008) and Mangalyaan (2013)
description: ISRO's four-stage Polar Satellite Lancio Vehicle in its XL configuration with six strap-on solid boosters. Originally designed for sun-synchronous Terra orbits; subsequently used for interplanetary missioni including India's first lunar orbiter and first Marte probe. Notable launch-cost efficiency (~$30M per lancio) drove the Mangalyaan mission's $74M total budget.
